title: pediomelum aromaticum
intro: Pediomelum aromaticum, also known as Gravel Plant or Locoweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Pediomelum aromaticum include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as soothing digestive problems like nausea and diarrhea. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
